entry_types.description: Benchmark-operated runs executed under the frozen official profile and official reporting window.
requirements: must use the frozen official evaluation profile
must remain inside the frozen reporting window
must publish repeat count, contamination flag, cost, and runtime summary
may keep full hidden traces private while publishing redacted summaries

entry_types.description: Externally produced runs that follow the frozen window rules but are not benchmark-operated.
requirements: must publish repeat count, cost, and runtime summary
must never be merged into the hosted leaderboard table for the same window

entry_types.description: Maintainer-published benchmark reference systems used to anchor interpretation rather than crowd rankings.
requirements: must identify runner family and benchmark version explicitly
must be rerun when the official window changes
must be labeled as reference rather than open submission

response_actions: do not clear a contaminated entry silently; attach or update the public contamination flag
invalidate entries that rely on evaluator bugs, hidden-scenario leakage, or benchmark-specific exploit loops
re-score or retire prior entries when an adjudicated evaluator bug materially changes leaderboard meaning
review_steps: record the contamination or exploit signal in the governance log
compare the signal against the relevant hidden pack, evaluator, and run-manifest evidence
decide whether the entry remains reportable, moves to a flagged table, or is retired
publish the resolution in the public changelog or release note
public_disclosure_rules: all public tables must show the active contamination flag
retired or invalidated entries should remain visible in history with a reason
contamination notes must distinguish suspected exposure from confirmed leakage

publication_triggers: opening a new official evaluation window
publishing a new hosted result set
changing the contamination state of an entry
retiring a pack, window, or leaderboard entry
minimum_fields: reason for any retirement or invalidation

entry_retirement_triggers: confirmed exploit use or benchmark-policy violation
material evaluator bug that invalidates the published ranking meaning
window closure followed by incompatible benchmark-version migration
pack_retirement_rules: retire hidden packs when contamination risk becomes material or when a fresh successor is promoted
record the retirement in the public pack changelog with contamination status and notes
do not accept new leaderboard entries against a retired official pack without an explicit legacy table boundary
benchmark_version_boundaries: do not merge leaderboard tables across incompatible major benchmark versions
treat each official evaluation window as its own reporting boundary
carry forward historical entries only with clear legacy labeling

notes: This governance pack is intentionally pre-human-calibration and should not be mistaken for a final public leaderboard constitution.
The window and governance pack should evolve together rather than drifting independently.
